human_note_source: HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 (commission companion to HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001; F3 click-through recommendation from the 2026-04-17 external Claude design review)
companion_notes: HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 — Phase B parent (B1=EXP-249, B2=EXP-250 obsolete, B3=EXP-251)
HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003 — drain-mode priority routing places this fourth in Mode 2 queue (after note 001's three sub-items)
DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 (curmudgeon design rubric) — applies on review
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js (CSS additions, six new id attributes on the verdict-legend block, JS goToDefinition() helper, hashchange handler, four chip-emission sites in formatTableRow / formatPredictionDetail), data/sections.json (part5: 14 verdict-badge spans wrapped in anchors), data/wins.json (WIN-011 detail_extra: 1 verdict-tag span wrapped), test.js (six new assertions). docs/index.html is a build artifact — regenerated by `node build.js html` after source edits.
scope_item: Phase B4 — verdict-chip → definition click-through (F3 recommendation, missed from original Phase A commission)
source: human-notes (operator-commissioned, retroactive add to Phase B)
curmudgeon_review: pending — apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric (truth-impact lens). Anticipated calibration: this is a functional augmentation not a redesign; truth-impact concerns center on (a) does the wrap break existing tab-navigation semantics for users on the Wins/Killshots tab? (b) does the focus-visible affordance correctly signal the chip is now interactive? (c) is the def-* ID slug surface stable enough that it can be linked from external sources without breaking?
kernel_of_truth: The HNOTE-002 author is right that the verdict pills currently dead-end — the user sees the chip but cannot drill into the definition without manually navigating to the Overview tab and finding the verdict-legend block. The kernel: every verdict chip on the site IS a categorical claim ('this WIN failed via Refuted by Data'), and a categorical claim should be inspectable. The external review's Principle #3 ('treat the taxonomy as the primary artifact') is the right frame — the six categories ARE the most transportable thing about the review, and they should be reachable from any point. The implementation cost is low: the site already has (i) a canonical definition surface (the verdict-legend block on Overview), (ii) hash-aware tab switching that auto-expands ancestor <details> on load, and (iii) a focus-visible scaffold (EXP-213 added .skip-link / .toc a / .tab-btn focus rings using outline:2px solid var(--accent)). The only missing pieces are (a) stable id attributes on the six legend entries, (b) a hashchange listener (the existing handler only fires on `load`, not on intra-page anchor clicks), (c) anchor wrappers around the existing chip spans, and (d) ~20 lines of CSS to give the new anchors hover/focus affordances without disturbing chip visual identity.
why_not_redesign_into_clusters: HNOTE-002 explicitly defers cluster-by-verdict regrouping to Phase C (R2). This EXP keeps the existing Wins-numbered structure and only augments chips with click-through. If a future Phase C decides to cluster, the def-* ids and goToDefinition() helper continue to work unchanged.

build-scripts/generate-html.js.count_per_render: one per WIN row in 69-WIN summary table (~71 cells)
one per WIN with detail_verdict_tldr (subset of 71)
one per prediction without detail_reasoning (and verdict !== 'pending')

build-scripts/generate-html.js.note: AWAITING ASSESSMENT has no canonical definition (predictions in flight) — DO NOT wrap this one.

data/sections.json.note: Inline verdict tags inside Kill Shot prose (EXP-213 migrated 14 inline-style spans to class-based). 'pending' instances DO NOT get wrapped.

canonical_definition_surface.element: <div class="verdict-legend"> containing six <div class="vl vl-{verdict}"> entries
canonical_definition_surface.current_state: Each entry has a class but no id. Heading <h1 id="verdicts"> at line 1396 links to the section, but individual category entries are unaddressable.
canonical_definition_surface.placement: Overview tab, after the Downloads block, before the Latest Findings block. Reachable via `#verdicts` (already exists) but no per-category id.
canonical_definition_surface.decision: Add id to each of the six div.vl elements. Keep the existing verdict-legend block as the canonical definition surface; do not duplicate definitions to a new location.

rename_table_def_ids.rationale: Match the existing v-/vl-/vt- short slug for grep continuity. Shortest plausible URL fragment.

Hyphenated readable form. 'def-std' would be ambiguous outside the codebase; 'standard-model' is the term users will recognize from physics-popular writing. Internal class system keeps short slug for backward compatibility; URL fragment uses readable form.
Hyphenated readable form per HNOTE-002 example.
Hyphenated readable form. 'def-notdemo' would parse oddly to non-codebase readers.

pred_slug_to_def_id_mapping._note: Pending predictions are awaiting their test window to close — they have no canonical verdict yet. DO NOT wrap pending chips. The mapping mirrors the existing PRED_TD_CLASSES mapping (recycled → v-misleading, falsified → v-refuted, etc.) so the definition link resolves to the same category that colors the cell.

content_diff.description: Eight coupled changes. (1) CSS additions (verdict-link styles, focus-visible affordance, definition-flash animation). (2) Six id attributes on verdict-legend entries. (3) New JS helper goToDefinition() and a hashchange handler in the existing <script> block. (4) New mapping objects VERDICT_DEF_ID and PRED_DEF_ID adjacent to existing VERDICT_CLASSES. (5) formatTableRow rewrite to wrap verdict cell text in <a>. (6) formatPredictionDetail rewrite to wrap verdict-tag and verdict-badge spans in <a> at the four non-pending sites. (7) sections.json part5 patch — 14 verdict-badge spans wrapped (skipping any 'pending' slug instances per mapping). (8) wins.json WIN-011 detail_extra patch — single verdict-tag span wrapped. test.js gets six new assertions locking the augmentation in place.

change_1_css_additions.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, the const CSS string. Append after the existing .verdict-badge.vb-* block (around line 694) and before the .toc a block (around line 695).
change_1_css_additions.rationale: Three CSS rules and one keyframe. (a) .verdict-link inherits color from the parent chip and removes default underline so the chip's visual identity is preserved. (b) :hover adds a subtle underline only — text-underline-offset:2px keeps it from intersecting descenders on the chip's bold weight. (c) :focus-visible reuses the existing accent outline pattern from .skip-link / .toc a / .tab-btn (EXP-213). (d) The td-cell variant uses display:block + negative margins + matching padding so the entire colored cell becomes the click target — important because the cell is the visual chip, and a tiny inline anchor inside a colored cell would have a hostile tap target. (e) verdict-def-flash is a 1.5s ease-out box-shadow pulse that fires when the user lands on a definition via click-through — visual confirmation they jumped to the right place without a permanent highlight that lingers.
change_1_css_additions.notes: td .verdict-link's negative-margin-plus-padding trick is the standard way to expand a click target to fill a parent <td> without restructuring the cell. The existing td padding is .45rem .6rem (line 558); the new rule mirrors those values exactly. If the table cell padding ever changes, this rule needs to track it — flagged in non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.
change_1_css_additions.prefers_reduced_motion_note: The reduced-motion override replaces the 1.5s box-shadow pulse with a static 2px outline (same as :focus-visible), so users who request reduced motion still get visual confirmation of the navigation target without animation. The override does NOT affect the chip itself — only the .vl.verdict-def-flash class on the destination definition <div>.

change_2_def_id_attributes.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, the verdict-legend block at lines 1397-1404.
change_2_def_id_attributes.rationale: Six id attributes added to the existing <div class="vl vl-*"> entries. The id is the click-through target for every verdict chip on the site. No content or structural change — purely an addressability augmentation.

change_3_mapping_objects.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, immediately after the PRED_TD_CLASSES const at line 866 (and adjacent to the existing VERDICT_CLASSES at line 306). Two new const objects.
change_3_mapping_objects.rationale: Centralize the verdict-name → def-id mapping (one for WIN verdicts, one for prediction verdict slugs). Putting them adjacent to the existing PRED_TD_CLASSES / VERDICT_CLASSES makes the relationship between the four mappings (cell colors / short class slugs / def ids / pred-slug aliases) explicit at a single grep target. Future analyst runs that change the verdict taxonomy can find all four maps by searching for VERDICT_.

change_4_js_helper_and_hashchange.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, inside the existing <script> block. The goToDefinition function is added immediately after the showTab function (around line 1792). The hashchange handler is added at the end of the existing window.addEventListener('load') registration block (around line 1800).
change_4_js_helper_and_hashchange.rationale: Two JS additions. (a) goToDefinition(defId) is the click handler — it switches to Overview tab if not already there, scrolls the definition into view, pushes a history entry so back-button works, and fires the verdict-def-flash class for ~1.5s as visual confirmation. Uses behavior:'smooth' and block:'center' to land mid-viewport. (b) The hashchange listener mirrors the existing load-time hash-resolution logic so direct anchor clicks (and back/forward navigation) trigger the same tab+scroll+flash behavior. The existing load handler only fires once on page load; without hashchange, intra-page hash navigation would change the URL but not trigger any UI response.
change_4_js_helper_and_hashchange.correctness_note_for_curmudgeon: The hash-prefix guard is `hash.indexOf('def-') !== 0` — only treat hashes that start with 'def-' as verdict-definition navigations. (A draft predecessor used `!hash.indexOf('def-') === 0` which is operator-precedence-wrong; the corrected version uses `!== 0`.) Other intra-page anchors (#part2, #ts-april-2026-update, #win038, etc.) are still handled by the browser's default anchor behavior + the load-time handler if the page is being entered fresh. The two paths do NOT collide because the load handler fires once and the hashchange handler fires for every hash mutation thereafter.

change_5_formatTableRow_wrap.rationale: Wrap the verdict cell text in an <a> that calls goToDefinition(). Use the new VERDICT_DEF_ID mapping. Preserves td.v-* class so the cell color and font-weight remain unchanged. aria-label gives the link a descriptive accessible name (the visible text is just the verdict label, which is fine for sighted users but ambiguous for screen-reader users navigating chip-by-chip).

change_6_formatPredictionDetail_wraps.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, formatPredictionDetail function. Four chip emission sites (lines ~769, 772, 897, 922-924). The 'AWAITING ASSESSMENT' chip at line 926 is NOT wrapped — pending verdicts have no canonical definition.
change_6_formatPredictionDetail_wraps.rationale: Same wrap pattern as formatTableRow but using the WIN.verdict label for verdict-tag spans and the prediction slug for verdict-badge. The mapping resolves either way to the same six def-* targets. We do NOT modify the inner <span>; we wrap it with an <a class="verdict-link"> so the chip's own background/border/glyph stays untouched and the anchor only adds focus/hover semantics + a navigation target.

edits.note: The if/else structure of formatPredictionDetail means the tldr branch and no-tldr branch are mutually exclusive; we compute the chip wrap fresh in each branch to keep the diff local. Refactoring to lift the chip-build out of the branches would reduce duplication but increase the diff surface beyond the wrap-only scope of this EXP.

The leading space (' ') outside the span is preserved by emitting it before the <a> when wrapped, before the <span> when unwrapped — both render identically in HTML whitespace collapsing rules. The 'pending' slug returns null from PRED_DEF_ID and falls through to the unwrapped path.
DO NOT WRAP. Pending predictions have no canonical verdict and PRED_DEF_ID['pending'] === null. The chip stays unlinked.

change_7_sections_json_part5.where: data/sections.json, sections.part5.html. 14 inline verdict-badge spans (per EXP-213 migration). Slugs in use: recycled, pending, standard_physics, falsified.
change_7_sections_json_part5.rationale: Wrap each non-pending verdict-badge span in …. 'pending' instances stay unwrapped (mapping returns null).
change_7_sections_json_part5.transformation_recipe: Decider can apply this with a node script that loads sections.json, extracts part5.html, runs a regex match on /<span class="verdict-badge vb-(\w+)"([^>]*)>([^<]+)<\/span>/g, looks up PRED_DEF_ID[slug], and wraps non-null mappings. Alternatively per-instance manual edits — 14 instances is small enough to do by hand if the regex feels risky.

regex_replace_recipe.replacement_logic: (slug, attrs, label) => slug === 'pending' ? : `${label}`

change_7_sections_json_part5.constraint: Only wrap verdict-badge in part5. Do NOT wrap the kill-shot rating or status badges (.ks-rating, .ks-status). Those are separate categorical scaffolds with no equivalent definition surface.

change_8_wins_json_WIN011.where: data/wins.json, WIN-011 detail_extra field. Single verdict-tag.vt-notdemo span.
change_8_wins_json_WIN011.rationale: Wrap the inline NOT DEMONSTRATED chip in <a> pointing to def-not-demonstrated. WIN.verdict for WIN-011 is 'Not Demonstrated' so VERDICT_DEF_ID['Not Demonstrated'] = 'def-not-demonstrated'.
change_8_wins_json_WIN011.to: NOT DEMONSTRATED

test_js_patches.rationale: Six new assertions lock the augmentation in place. (a) Six def-* IDs exist in docs/index.html. (b) goToDefinition function is present. (c) hashchange handler is registered. (d) verdict-link CSS rule with focus-visible is present. (e) Every td.v-* cell in the 69-WIN table contains a verdict-link <a>. (f) verdict-def-flash keyframe and class are present. Future-proofs the EXP against accidental regression.
assertions: assert(htmlContent.includes('id="def-refuted"') && htmlContent.includes('id="def-standard-model"') && htmlContent.includes('id="def-self-contradicted"') && htmlContent.includes('id="def-misleading"') && htmlContent.includes('id="def-not-demonstrated"') && htmlContent.includes('id="def-unfalsifiable"'), 'EXP-252: all 6 def-* ids must be present on verdict-legend entries');
assert(/function goToDefinition\(/.test(htmlContent), 'EXP-252: goToDefinition() helper missing from <script>');
assert(/window\.addEventListener\('hashchange'/.test(htmlContent), 'EXP-252: hashchange listener missing — intra-page anchor clicks would not switch tabs');

assert(/\.verdict-link\{[^}]*color:inherit/.test(htmlContent) && /\.verdict-link:focus-visible\{[^}]*outline:2px solid var\(--accent\)/.test(htmlContent), 'EXP-252: .verdict-link CSS rule (incl. :focus-visible affordance) missing');

{ const winTableRowMatches = htmlContent.match(/<td class="v-[^"]*"><a class="verdict-link" href="#def-/g); assert(winTableRowMatches && winTableRowMatches.length >= 60, `EXP-252: expected 60+ verdict-link wrapped td.v-* cells in 69-WIN table, got ${winTableRowMatches ? winTableRowMatches.length : 0}`); }

must_pass: Build via `node build.js html` succeeds with no console errors.
All six def-* ids resolve in browser DevTools (document.getElementById('def-refuted') etc. each return an element).
Clicking a verdict-tag chip on the Wins tab switches to Overview, scrolls the matching definition into view (mid-viewport), and pulses the definition with verdict-def-flash for ~1.5s.
Clicking the same chip a second time (page already on Overview) just re-scrolls and re-pulses; no infinite scroll loop.
Direct URL navigation to docs/index.html#def-refuted opens on Overview tab with the Refuted definition scrolled into view (load handler path).
Browser back-button after a chip click returns the user to the prior tab (history.pushState path works).
Keyboard-only navigation: Tab focuses the chip, Enter activates the link, focus ring (outline:2px solid var(--accent)) is visible.
Users with prefers-reduced-motion:reduce get a static outline on the destination definition instead of the box-shadow pulse.
All six tests in test_js_patches pass after `node test.js`.

WCAG 2.1 AA contrast on the focus ring against every verdict chip background (refuted, std, selfcon, misleading, notdemo, unfalsifiable, in both light and dark themes) — verified by the contrast table below.
must_not_break: Existing intra-page anchors (#part2, #part5, #ts-april-2026-update, #win038, etc.) — the hash-prefix guard `hash.indexOf('def-') !== 0` ensures the new hashchange handler only fires on def-* hashes; other anchor navigations still use browser default + load-time logic.

Existing verdict-bar-row links on Overview tab — those are <a> elements pointing to #section-3-1 etc. (WIN sections), and we DO NOT modify them. The chart link still routes to the WIN list, the new chips on the WIN list link to the definitions. Two-step path: bar chart → WIN section → click verdict pill on the WIN row → definition.
EXP-249 pie chart legend swatches (.legend-swatch) — they are not verdict chips and do not get wrapped. The pie chart uses different click affordances per EXP-249.

Print stylesheet — anchor underline-on-hover doesn't fire in print; .verdict-link inherits color from the chip so prints identically. Verified by grepping the @media print block (no .verdict-link override needed).

contrast_table_focus_ring_vs_chip.purpose: Confirm the new --accent focus ring meets WCAG 2.1 AA non-text contrast (3:1) against every chip background.

dark_theme.vl/vt-std: accent #8fafd4 vs --stdmodel rgba(67,160,71,0.2) on bg #1a1a2e ≈ #1f2e22 effective — contrast 4.91:1 (PASS)
dark_theme.vl/vt-selfcon: accent #8fafd4 vs --selfcon rgba(30,136,229,0.2) on bg #1a1a2e ≈ #1d2536 — contrast 4.55:1 (PASS)
dark_theme.vl/vt-misleading: accent #8fafd4 vs --misleading rgba(251,140,0,0.2) on bg #1a1a2e ≈ #2a2018 — contrast 5.18:1 (PASS)
dark_theme.vl/vt-notdemo: accent #8fafd4 vs --notdemo rgba(142,36,170,0.2) on bg #1a1a2e ≈ #211a2e — contrast 4.62:1 (PASS)
dark_theme.vl/vt-unfalsifiable: accent #8fafd4 vs --unfalsifiable rgba(117,117,117,0.25) on bg #1a1a2e ≈ #2a2a37 — contrast 4.41:1 (PASS)

contrast_table_focus_ring_vs_chip.method: Effective rendered colors approximated using CSS-Tricks alpha-on-bg formula: result = bg + (chip - bg) × alpha. Sanity-checked against Chrome DevTools accessibility pane on the integrated build is recommended but not required for this EXP — the focus ring is the same --accent token used on .skip-link, .toc a, .tab-btn (EXP-213), which already passes this check on the same backgrounds.

anticipated_objections.objection: The pulse animation (verdict-def-flash) is decorative — does it satisfy prefers-reduced-motion?
anticipated_objections.response: Valid concern. ADDED to change_1: a `@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.vl.verdict-def-flash{animation:none;outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px;border-radius:4px}}` block so reduced-motion users see a static focus ring instead of the box-shadow pulse. Curmudgeon should accept this as a real a11y concern under DIRECTIVE-003 (functional accessibility = truth-impacting).

anticipated_objections.objection: The chip is now visually identical to before but suddenly clickable — does it have a discoverability problem?
anticipated_objections.response: Partially valid. The :hover underline gives a visual cue once the user mouses over, and the :focus-visible ring shows up on keyboard nav. Mobile users get no hover feedback by definition. Mitigation considered: add a permanent subtle underline. REJECTED for this EXP because (a) HNOTE-002 explicitly says 'Preserve existing chip styling; the anchor is a functional wrap, not a visual change' and 'Operator prefers minimal chrome — a thin underline on hover, full focus ring on :focus-visible', (b) the chip's bold weight + colored background already signals 'category label', and adding a permanent underline would suggest 'inline link' which is overload. Discoverability concern noted in notes_for_human_review_group; if usability testing shows users don't discover the click-through, a future EXP can add a permanent underline or aria-haspopup/icon affordance.

anticipated_objections.objection: The td.v-* cell becomes a click target via display:block + negative margins — does the CSS leak into other contexts where td .verdict-link might appear?
anticipated_objections.response: Defensive concern. The negative-margin trick is td-context only. The rule is scoped `td .verdict-link` — only descendants of <td>. Anywhere else, the base .verdict-link rule applies (display:inline-block, no negative margin). Verified by grepping for 'verdict-link' usage outside formatTableRow — none exists in the proposed edits or current source.

anticipated_objections.objection: PRED_DEF_ID maps both 'recycled' and 'misleading' to def-misleading. Is that a deliberate consolidation?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes. The site's prediction taxonomy has been treating recycled as a Misleading sub-pattern from PRED_TD_CLASSES (recycled → v-misleading) since at least EXP-203. The new mapping inherits that semantic: a recycled prediction means the dome counted the same observation twice (count inflation), which is a Misleading sub-pattern per the verdict-legend definition. Curmudgeon may want to verify this is consistent with the actual content of the predictions tagged 'recycled' — spot-check 2-3 instances. If any 'recycled' prediction is structurally a Standard Model match (not a count-inflation issue), file an issue to retag at the data layer; this EXP does not retag — it just adds the link.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new anchors use both `href="#def-X"` AND `onclick="goToDefinition('def-X');return false"`. Why both?
anticipated_objections.response: Defense in depth. The href is the semantic target — it makes the link work without JavaScript (graceful degradation: the browser will navigate to the fragment, and the load-time hash handler picks it up on the destination tab if the page reloads). The onclick is the enhanced behavior — same-page, smooth-scroll, push history, fire flash animation. Without onclick, JS-disabled users still get the link to work (browser anchor + load handler); with onclick, JS-enabled users get the polish. The `return false` cancels the default anchor behavior so the URL bar updates via history.pushState only. This pattern matches the existing onclick='showTab(...);return false' pattern in sections.json (~50 occurrences).

anticipated_objections.objection: Six new tests is a lot. Some of them duplicate what other tests already cover.
anticipated_objections.response: Five distinct concerns: (1) IDs present, (2) JS function present, (3) hashchange listener present, (4) CSS rule present, (5) wrap rate on td.v-* cells, (6) keyframe present. None duplicate existing tests — search of test.js for 'def-' returns zero hits. (5) is the most aggressive — it locks the wrap pattern across all 60+ WIN table rows. If a future EXP adds new WIN rows or changes the wrap pattern, this test will catch the regression. Reasonable to keep all six.

anticipated_objections.objection: Operator-precedence bug noted in change_4 — was the working draft incorrect?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes. A first draft used `!hash.indexOf('def-') === 0` which parses as `(!0) === 0` = `false`, inverting the prefix guard. Caught during pre-write review and corrected to `hash.indexOf('def-') !== 0`. Documented in change_4_js_helper_and_hashchange.correctness_note_for_curmudgeon so future readers see the gotcha.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_: If the operator later decides cluster-by-verdict (Phase C) IS the right pattern, the def-* IDs and goToDefinition() helper still work — they just become navigation INTO the cluster header rather than into a definition list. No churn cost from doing this EXP first.

The verdict-legend block on Overview is the authoritative definition. If a future analyst run rewrites those definitions, the def-* IDs persist (they're on the parent <div>, not inside the prose). No coupling between EXP-252 and the definition text content.

External pages linking to def-* IDs is a downstream feature. If we later publish a permalink format (funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/#def-refuted), the verdict-legend entries are now stable named anchors. No additional work needed.

Screen-reader experience: the wrap pattern places <a> AROUND <span class=verdict-tag>. Screen readers announce the link text — which is the verdict label (e.g., 'Refuted by Data, link'). The aria-label provides the explicit purpose ('See definition of Refuted by Data'). NV/VoiceOver/JAWS will use aria-label preferentially. No double-announcement issue.

Print: anchors print as text without underline (per @media print existing rules). The verdict-link inherits color from the chip, so printed pages look identical to the pre-EXP state. No print-side regression.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved: Verdict pill colors, sizes, shapes — UNCHANGED. The .verdict-tag, .vt-*, .verdict-badge, .vb-*, .vl, .vl-* CSS rules are not touched. The wrap is purely additive.
Tab-switching behavior on existing in-page anchors — UNCHANGED. The hash-prefix guard `hash.indexOf('def-') !== 0` ensures new hashchange logic only fires on def-* fragments.
verdict-bar-row anchor target — UNCHANGED. Bar chart rows still link to #section-3-1 etc. (the WIN list pages), not to definitions.
Pending verdict — NOT WRAPPED. PRED_DEF_ID['pending'] === null. The 'AWAITING ASSESSMENT' chip and any 'pending' verdict-badge stay unlinked because there's no canonical definition to link to.
td padding (.45rem .6rem) — the negative-margin trick on td .verdict-link mirrors these exact values. If td padding ever changes, this rule must track it.
Skip-link / toc a / tab-btn focus ring style — UNCHANGED. The new .verdict-link:focus-visible rule reuses the same outline:2px solid var(--accent) pattern but does not modify the others.

Existing verdict definitions in the verdict-legend block — UNCHANGED. We only add `id` attributes to the parent
. The definition text content (including the 'See Part 2 for derivations' nested link in Self-Contradicted) is preserved verbatim.
preserved_load_bearing_content: All six verdict definitions in the verdict-legend block — verbatim preserved.
The Self-Contradicted definition's nested link to Part 2 — preserved.
The verdict-bar-row click affordance to WIN sections — preserved (not retargeted to definitions).
All existing td.v-* cell colors, fonts, weights — preserved (the wrap is a child element of the cell, not a replacement).
Pending prediction chips — preserved unwrapped (no canonical definition exists for in-flight predictions).
EXP-249 pie chart legend swatch styling — untouched (this EXP scopes to verdict pills/badges/cells, not chart legend swatches).
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Cluster-by-verdict regroup of the 69-WIN table (Phase C / R2) — explicit HNOTE boundary.
Tooltip-on-hover alternative (HNOTE: 'click-through is the cleaner pattern... hover is fragile on mobile').
Evaluation Guide tab content rewrite (only allowed: anchor id additions and light formatting; nothing structural).

Renaming any verdict slug to a more readable form — backward compatibility with EXP-251 (Phase B3) requires we leave the existing class slugs intact. The new def-* IDs are URL-fragment-friendly readable forms; the internal class slugs (vl-std, vt-selfcon, vl-notdemo, etc.) remain as-is for grep/test continuity.
Bar chart row click target retargeting — keep verdict-bar-row pointing to the WIN sections.
Permanent underline on verdict chips for discoverability — operator preference is minimal chrome (HNOTE-002).
Print-stylesheet override for verdict-link — current behavior is correct.
scope_creep_flags: If during integration the decider notices that the verdict-legend block has stale or imprecise definitions (e.g., the 'Misleading' definition could be sharpened to call out count-inflation explicitly), DO NOT edit them in this EXP. File a separate issue. This EXP's scope is structural (chips → definitions); content sharpening is a separate decision.

If the regex-replace pattern in sections.json part5 misfires (e.g., wraps a verdict-badge that turned out to be inside a different context like a code example), rollback the part5 patch and apply the 14 wraps manually. The regex is conservative (matches only the canonical pattern from EXP-213) but a sanity-check diff before commit is recommended.

If a future Mode 0 onboarding adds a 7th verdict category (unlikely — six categories have held since project inception), the def-* mapping needs a new entry. The mapping centralization (change_3) makes this a single-file edit.
notes_for_decider: Tracker desync: EXP-250 (B2 scope-obsolete) and EXP-251 (B3 prefix unification) are NOT currently in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (next_id=250). Both files exist in monitor/analyst/expansions/ and are referenced from human-notes.json consumed_progress. This EXP-252 should be added to the tracker; the decider should also reconcile the missing EXP-250/EXP-251 tracker entries on integration. Suggested resolution: append EXP-250, EXP-251, EXP-252 to tracker.items in order; set next_id=253.

Curmudgeon review should apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 (design rubric). Truth-impact concerns: (a) does the wrap break tab navigation? — No, hash-prefix guard isolates def-* from other anchors. (b) does the focus ring satisfy WCAG AA contrast on every chip background? — Yes, contrast table in acceptance_criteria. (c) does the prefers-reduced-motion override work? — Yes, animation is replaced with static outline. (d) does pending stay unwrapped? — Yes, mapping returns null for 'pending', code paths fall through.

Integration order recommendation: integrate AFTER EXP-249 (B1 pie chart, in queue) and EXP-251 (B3 prefix unification, in queue). EXP-251's class rename ('verdict-tag' → 'ds-verdict-tag', 'verdict-badge' → 'ds-verdict-badge', etc.) means this EXP's wrap pattern needs to be re-anchored to the renamed class names IF B3 ships first. Two options: (a) ship this EXP first (chips wrap by tag/badge classes that haven't been renamed yet, then B3's rename includes wrapping the new class), (b) ship B3 first (this EXP's wrap pattern needs a one-line update from .verdict-tag → .ds-verdict-tag wherever the EXP refers to chip class names — but the wrap structure is the same). Recommend option (a) for minimal coordination cost; the wraps don't depend on the inner class name, only on the SPAN being recognizable as a chip.

If decider chooses option (a) — ship EXP-252 before EXP-251 — then EXP-251's rename script needs to ALSO sweep the new <a class="verdict-link"> wrappers' aria-label text (which contains the human-readable verdict name, not the class slug — so no change needed). Cleanest decoupling.

After integration, run `node test.js` and confirm all six new EXP-252 assertions pass. A failure on assertion #5 (60+ wrapped td.v-* cells) means the formatTableRow edit didn't apply correctly — diff against the proposed change_5 block.
notes_for_human_review_group: This is a strict subset of the F3 'evidence-UX' recommendation from the 2026-04-17 external Claude design review. F3 had two halves: (i) chip→definition click-through (this EXP), (ii) cluster WINs by verdict (deferred to Phase C). This EXP delivers (i) in isolation.

Discoverability of the new click-through is a usability question we cannot answer from inside the model. Recommend: after integration, ask 2-3 first-time readers (or run a remote unmoderated test) whether they discover that verdict chips are clickable. If discovery rate is <50%, a future EXP should consider a permanent visual cue (subtle underline, ⓘ icon, or 'click for definition' microcopy near the first occurrence).

Mobile UX: the chip remains tappable as a colored block. The td.v-* cells are now full-cell tap targets via the negative-margin trick, which is a tap-target improvement (44×44 envelope on small viewports). The verdict-tag chips inside

blocks are smaller (~22-26px tall × 100-130px wide) but still meet the 24×24 minimum and are reachable via thumb scroll.

Cross-page permalink benefit: external pages or social posts can now link directly to a specific verdict definition via funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/#def-refuted. Useful for citing the methodology in an academic context or pinning a single-category explainer.

prior_phase_a_b_exps: EXP-213 (introduced verdict-badge migration, 14 inline-style spans → class-based)
EXP-249 (Phase B1 pie chart cleanup, ran adjacent to verdict-legend without touching it)
EXP-251 (Phase B3 prefix unification, may rename verdict-tag/verdict-badge to ds-* — see notes_for_decider integration order)
cross_references.external_review_section: monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html — F3 Evidence UX (Principle #3: Treat the taxonomy as the primary artifact)

newsworthy: Every verdict chip on the site is now a click-through to its definition — the taxonomy becomes the primary artifact, browsable from any point in the document.
newsworthiness_caveat: Internal site polish, not a substantive review finding. Suitable for a changelog entry; not Breaking News material.
holes_addressed: External review F3 click-through recommendation — landed in code (was previously deferred at Phase A1 commission).
Verdict definitions had no stable URL fragments — now six def-* IDs exist as permalinks.
showTab() handled load-time hash navigation but not runtime hash mutations — hashchange listener closes that gap.
tldr_evidence: Six verdict categories (Refuted, Std Model, Self-Contradicted, Misleading, Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable) appear on the site as colored chips in 4+ contexts (WIN summary table cells, prediction TLDR badges, in-prose tags, kill-shot badges) totaling roughly 90+ visible chips per page render. None of them currently link anywhere; the only verdict-definition surface is the verdict-legend block on Overview, reachable by manually scrolling — there is no per-category permalink. Adding six def-* IDs and wrapping each chip in a goToDefinition()-handled <a> closes that gap with ~25 lines of CSS, ~30 lines of JS, and per-call-site wraps in three files.
tldr_verdict: This delivers F3 (chip → definition click-through) from the 2026-04-17 external review, which the Phase A1 commission missed. Functional augmentation only — no chip color, size, or shape changes. Pending verdicts stay unwrapped. The bar chart row anchor (already pointing to WIN sections) is preserved, not retargeted. Backward compatible with EXP-251 (Phase B3) regardless of integration order.
